entries.claim: Overlapping OAuth, refresh, and turn reasons share one foreground service until the last bounded lease ends.
The default APK starts and stops the declared dataSync network-critical foreground service on the Pixel.
Connectivity preflight selects a closed compiled target for every provider and purpose pair.
Provider transport uses separate compiled response, idle-read, and overall turn deadlines.
The preflight route requires session/capability gates, strict bounded JSON, and no-store responses.
Mobile connectivity snapshots are opaque one-shot handles bound to the engine session and closed guard reason.
Status preserves the selected Codex provider and reports refresh_required without performing token refresh.
Credential refresh uses an injected clock and produces a complete finite credential before persistence.
A deliberately incomplete refresh response returns reconnect-required and cannot replace the last complete encrypted product credential.
Concurrent refreshes for one provider are serialized.
A blocked Codex callback accept cannot outlive the owning attempt deadline.
The current default APK completed official system-browser Codex OAuth while the OAuth guard remained active and stored an encrypted product credential.
entries.command: Physical Pixel product OAuth through the app, system browser, callback listener, and closed status readback under device-lock ag-640

entries.claim: A controlled read-only StoreArchive turn completed once while the default APK was backgrounded and the foreground guard remained active until terminal completion.
entries.command: Physical Pixel default-APK controlled background streamed turn with closed CDP event counters and foreground-service readback

entries.claim: The separate hook APK exposes an owner-only one-shot diagnostic hook and consumes it once.
The hook APK renders the closed no_validated_network diagnostic with Retry and Settings, consumes the hook, opens the system Internet panel, and creates no provider turn.
entries.command: Physical Pixel hook-APK one-shot no_validated_network UI probe followed by system-dialog focus and hook-consumption readback

entries.claim: The independently rebuilt default APK excludes the deliberate network diagnostic hook marker; credential persistence is proven separately before the later instrumentation cleanup.
entries.command: (cd android && env -u ISY_CARGO_FEATURES ./gradlew clean :app:assembleDebug) && python3 tools/agent-network-device-probe.py --apk android/app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk --scope default --output /tmp/isyncyou-640-default-device-probe.json

entries.claim: Production sources and feature graphs contain no callback diagnostic writer, fixed-IP/DoH routing residue, unsafe Settings action, idle callback listener, or default hook activation.
The current default APK completed the official system-browser Claude OAuth code flow and then completed a guarded read-only StoreArchive turn through the Claude product provider.
entries.command: Physical Pixel default-APK Claude OAuth code flow followed by a controlled no-match StoreArchive turn with closed event counters and foreground-service readback

entries.claim: A one-shot JNI-only hook marked the existing Codex product credential refresh-required without injecting time or credentials; the normal guarded refresh route performed a real provider refresh, atomically changed one encrypted credential file, and the clean default APK loaded it after restart and completed a guarded StoreArchive turn.
entries.command: Physical Pixel hook-APK one-shot refresh-due arming followed by normal guard, snapshot, product refresh route, metadata-only encrypted-store comparison, clean default-APK restart, and controlled StoreArchive turn
